title: Can we dissociate hypervigilance to social threats from altered perceptual decision-making processes in lonely individuals? An exploration with Drift Diffusion Modelling and event-related potentials.

affiliations.name: Social Neuroscience Lab, Institute of Psychology Polish Academy of Sciences  Warsaw Poland

description: This dataset investigates the cognitive and neural mechanisms underlying loneliness by examining whether hypervigilance to social threats can be dissociated from altered perceptual decision-making processes in lonely individuals. Using event-related potentials (ERP) and drift diffusion modeling, the study combines electrophysiological recordings with computational modeling of decision-making to elucidate the distinct contributions of threat sensitivity and perceptual processing to loneliness-related social cognition.
methods_description: Data collection involved event-related potential (ERP) recordings and behavioral measures of perceptual decision-making. Drift diffusion modeling was applied to decompose decision-making processes into their underlying cognitive components.
